---
title: ""
pagetitle: "Iago Mamede"
---

::: {.home-hero}
::: {.home-photo}
![](assets/img/home-stochastic-thermodynamics.png){fig-alt="Diagrammatic scientific schematic of stochastic thermodynamics with Brownian trajectories and hot and cold reservoirs"}
:::

::: {.home-hero-content}
<div class="home-name-lockup">
  <div class="home-main-name">Iago Mamede</div>
</div>

::: {.home-intro}
::: {.intro-copy}
<div class="intro-kicker">Physics · Stochastic thermodynamics</div>

## Nonequilibrium statistical physics, rare events and microscopic heat engines

I am a PhD student in Physics at the Universidade de São Paulo (Brazil), conducting part of my doctoral research at Hasselt University (Belgium) through the BOF-BILA program.

My research lies at the interface of stochastic thermodynamics, nonequilibrium statistical physics and statistical mechanics of interacting systems. I study how microscopic systems exchange heat, perform work and produce entropy when thermal fluctuations, collective effects and rare events play a central role.

I am particularly interested in stochastic heat engines, nonequilibrium phase transitions, interacting spin models and Brownian systems. My work combines analytical methods from master equations, stochastic processes and large-deviation theory with numerical simulations.
:::

::: {.research-panel}
## Research interests

- Stochastic thermodynamics
- Nonequilibrium statistical mechanics
- Interacting spin systems
- Stochastic heat engines
- Large-deviation theory
- Brownian dynamics
- Entropy production
- Optimal paths and rare events
:::
:::

:::
:::
